text.title: Rust Error Handling Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real rust-error-handling deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: Library returns Box<dyn Error>; downstream cannot match variants; consistent HTTP mapping impossible.
antipattern.root-cause: Author defaulted to dynamic error type at crate boundary.
antipattern.fix: thiserror enum at crate boundary; anyhow only at binary main.

antipattern.symptom: handler.rs calls .unwrap() on Result; panic crashes the request and emits a 500 with no detail.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated unwrap as 'should never fail' instead of a panic primitive.
antipattern.fix: CI denies clippy::unwrap_used + expect_used; return Result<_, AppError> from every fallible call.

antipattern.symptom: Library function returns anyhow::Result; consumers cannot destructure errors.
antipattern.root-cause: Author let anyhow leak from script into library API.
antipattern.fix: Library APIs return thiserror enum; convert anyhow → typed at the boundary.
